title: Max Storage Caster Dresser
summary: A six-drawer dresser on caster wheels, sized to modify easily — plywood carcass, precise inner framing for drawer alignment, and drawer faces attached with pulls pre-installed.
description:
This dresser is designed from the ground up to be resized, with a plywood carcass and inner framing rather than fixed 1x-lumber dimensions — the source's own emphasis is that this framing must be precise, since it's what every drawer opening measures against. Attach the top to the sides first, tacking with a nail before driving pocket screws to keep everything from shifting mid-assembly, then add the inner framing that actually defines each drawer bay.

The plywood back isn't just a finish panel here — it's used to square the whole carcass as it goes on, so glue it and let its factory-square corners pull the case true rather than trusting the frame alone. **Measure your own openings before building drawers**, confirming they match the diagram or adjusting the drawer box dimensions to fit what you actually built — a plywood carcass this size rarely lands on the calculated number in every opening.

Install the drawer slide cabinet member inset 7/8" into each opening, and level the whole cabinet in all directions before doing it — a slide installed square to a tilted cabinet still runs crooked. **Paint or stain the drawer faces before attaching hardware** — it's a small sequencing choice, but drilling pull holes on a flat, unattached face is far easier than drilling them once the face is mounted. Caster wheels on the base finish the piece, turning a heavy six-drawer dresser into something that can be repositioned without lifting it.

materials.name: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Edge band the front and end edges.
Edge band the front edge only.

cutList.material: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets

cutList.note: Measure your actual opening; should be about 2-1/2" less than opening width.
Measure your actual opening; should be about 2-1/2" less than opening width.

cutList.material: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one top at 60" and two sides at 31" from the 3/4" hardwood plywood, full sheet. Cut two bottoms at 58-1/2", two center rails at 30-1/4", four small drawer sides at 20" and four small drawer front/back at 26-3/8" from the 1x4. Cut four between-drawer trim at 28-7/8" from the 1x2. Cut one back panel at 31-3/4" from the 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets. Cut eight large drawer sides at 20" and eight large drawer front/back at 26-3/8" from the 1x8. Cut six drawer bottoms at 27-7/8" from the 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets. Cut two top drawer faces at 28-5/8" from the 1x6. Cut four lower drawer faces at 28-5/8" from the 1x12. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to five 8 ft 1x4, two 8 ft 1x2, five 8 ft 1x8, one 5 ft 1x6 and one 10 ft 1x12. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets

steps.title: Attach the top and sides
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the inside edge of the sides. Attach to the underside of the top with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws — first nail the top to the sides to hold in place, then secure with pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the inner framing
steps.body: Attach the inner framing (bottoms, center rails, between-drawer trim), building precisely — this must be accurate for the drawers to align. No trim is needed on the back.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Apply glue and attach the 1/4" plywood back, using its square factory corners to square up the carcass as you attach it.
materials: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Measure your drawer openings and confirm they match the diagram, adjusting as needed. Build the drawer boxes with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, attaching the bottom with 3/4" brad nails or staples and glue.
materials: 1/4" hardwood plywood or underlayment, full sheets

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Check that the cabinet is level in all directions. Install the drawer slide cabinet member inset 7/8" into each opening.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces
steps.body: Paint or stain the drawer faces first, and attach hardware before mounting the faces for easier drilling. Attach the faces to the drawer boxes with 1-1/4" brad nails, leaving an even 1/8" gap around all four sides. Further secure from the inside with 1-1/4" screws.

steps.title: Add handles and casters
steps.body: Attach handles to the drawer faces if not already done, and attach caster wheels to the base.
